auth_notes: Requires creating a 'Machine to Machine' application in the Auth0 dashboard, authorizing it for the Management API, and selecting specific scopes. Authentication involves a POST request to /oauth/token to retrieve a JWT bearer token.
rate_limits: Varies by tenant tier; typically 2 requests per second for Free tenants and up to 100 requests per second for Enterprise tenants on the Management API.
gotchas: The Management API has strict rate limits that can be easily hit during bulk user operations.
Access tokens for the Management API are short-lived; workflows must include logic to request a new token or use n8n's OAuth2 credentials feature carefully.
Scopes must be manually assigned to the M2M application for every specific entity (users, roles, logs) you intend to manage.
operations_available: Search Users by Email or Metadata
research_notes: Implementation requires an HTTP Request node to fetch the JWT token followed by subsequent HTTP Request nodes using 'Bearer Token' header. It is recommended to use a 'Wait' node or 'Split In Batches' when performing bulk user updates to respect rate limits. Webhooks can be implemented using Auth0 'Actions' or 'Log Streams' pointing to an n8n Webhook node.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Implementation requires an HTTP Request node to fetch the JWT token followed by subsequent HTTP Request nodes using 'Bearer Token' header. It is recommended to use a 'Wait' node or 'Split In Batches' when performing bulk user updates to respect rate limits.
